title: Mild Fluffy Chinese Zucchini Stir-Fry
description: A gentle, fluffy stirfry of zucchini with mild aromatics, ideal for a sick day and fully kosher.
instructions: Wash the zucchinis and trim the ends; cut into thin, wide ribbons with a vegetable peeler or julienne knife.
Heat the vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Add the minced ginger and garlic; sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in the zucchini ribbons, tossing quickly to coat with aromatics.
Add the sugar, white pepper, and optional chili flakes; stir for 1 minute.
Pour in the soy sauce and water or broth, then reduce heat to medium.
Cover the pan and let the zucchini steam for 3–4 minutes, until it becomes tender yet still crisp.
Drizzle the sesame oil over the vegetables, then stir in the diced scallions.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed; cook for another 30 seconds.
Transfer to a serving dish and serve immediately, optionally with steamed rice.
